name: octoparse_update_existing_template_task
description:
Updates an existing template-based task with new parameters, name, or group assignment. This is for modifying tasks that have already been created.

[When to use]:
- MUST call when user says "update task X", "change task Y parameters", "modify task Z"
- Call when user wants to rename an existing task
- Call when user wants to move a task to a different group
- Call when user wants to change template parameters (e.g., different search keywords)

[When NOT to use]:
- DO NOT call if user wants to create a NEW task (use createTemplateTask instead)
- DO NOT call if user wants to start/stop a task (use startCloudTask/stopCloudTask)
- DO NOT call if user just wants to view task info (use getTaskInfoById)

[CRITICAL - Prerequisites]:
Before calling this tool, you MUST:
1. Call getTaskInfoById(taskId) to get current task configuration
2. Preserve all existing values that user doesn't want to change
3. Call getTemplateView(templateId) to verify template version and parameters if changing parameters

[IMPORTANT - All Parameters Required]:
Unlike createTemplateTask, this tool requires ALL parameters because it performs a complete update.

[CRITICAL - userInputParameters Structure]:
The userInputParameters field is REQUIRED and must contain BOTH UIParameters and TemplateParameters arrays.

**UIParameters** (REQUIRED):
- Purpose: UI-level parameters for displaying in Octoparse Desktop client
- Format: Array of objects with structure: [{Id: "param_id", Value: "param_value"}]
- Each parameter MUST have: Id (string) - parameter identifier from template schema
- Each parameter MAY have: Value (any) - the parameter value
- When updating: Get current UIParameters from getTaskInfoById, modify only what needs to change

**TemplateParameters** (REQUIRED):
- Purpose: Template-level parameters for actual scraping execution
- Format: Array of objects with structure: [{ParamName: "param_name", Value: "param_value"}]
- Each parameter MUST have: ParamName (string) - parameter name from template
- Each parameter MAY have: Value (any) - the parameter value
- When updating: Get current TemplateParameters from getTaskInfoById, modify only what needs to change

**CRITICAL - Parameter Pairing Rule**:
Every parameter MUST appear in BOTH UIParameters and TemplateParameters arrays:
- If a parameter exists in UIParameters (by Id), it MUST also exist in TemplateParameters (by ParamName)
- If a parameter exists in TemplateParameters (by ParamName), it MUST also exist in UIParameters (by Id)
- UIParameters use Id for identification, TemplateParameters use ParamName
- Both arrays must contain the same parameters, just with different key names
- When modifying parameters, ensure you update BOTH arrays to keep them paired

**CRITICAL - Parameter Value Types Based on ControlType**:
The ControlType field in template parameters determines the expected Value format:

- **ControlType="MultiInput"**: Value MUST be an ARRAY
  * Used for parameters that accept multiple inputs (e.g., multiple keywords, multiple URLs)
  * Example: {Id: "param-id", Value: ["keyword1", "keyword2", "keyword3"]}
  * ❌ WRONG: {Id: "param-id", Value: "keyword1"} // Single string will cause errors
  * ✅ CORRECT: {Id: "param-id", Value: ["keyword1"]} // Array with one element
  * Even for single values, wrap in array: Value: ["single_value"]
  * When updating: If existing Value is a string but ControlType is MultiInput, convert to array!

- **ControlType="TextInput"** or **"SingleInput"**: Value should be a STRING
  * Example: {Id: "param-id", Value: "single_keyword"}

- **ControlType="NumberInput"**: Value should be a NUMBER
  * Example: {Id: "param-id", Value: 100}

- **ControlType="Checkbox"**: Value should be a BOOLEAN
  * Example: {Id: "param-id", Value: true}

**IMPORTANT**: When modifying parameters, check the template's ControlType definition to ensure correct Value format. MultiInput is the most common mistake - forgetting to wrap values in an array will cause task update to fail.

**Error Messages You Might See**:
- "UIParameters is required. Please provide an array of UI parameters with required fields: [{Id: "some_id", Value: "some_value"}]"
  -> You forgot to include UIParameters or it's empty
- "TemplateParameters is required. Please provide an array of template parameters with required fields: [{ParamName: "param_name", Value: "param_value"}]"
  -> You forgot to include TemplateParameters or it's empty
- Parameter mismatch error
  -> Parameters in UIParameters don't match those in TemplateParameters (not properly paired)

[Workflow]:
1. User: "Change task 123's search keyword to 'iPhone'"
2. getTaskInfoById(123) -> Get current configuration including userInputParameters
3. Extract current UIParameters and TemplateParameters
4. CHECK ControlType for the parameter being modified (from template definition)
5. Modify BOTH with CORRECT format:
   - Find parameter in UIParameters (by Id) and update Value (array if MultiInput!)
   - Find corresponding parameter in TemplateParameters (by ParamName) and update Value (array if MultiInput!)
6. update_existing_template_task with ALL fields (modified UIParameters + modified TemplateParameters + all other fields)

[Examples]:

Example 1: Update MultiInput parameter with single value (CORRECT way)
User: "Change search keyword in task abc123 to 'iPhone'"
-> 1. getTaskInfoById("abc123")
-> 2. getTemplateView(templateId) -> Check ControlType
-> 3. If ControlType="MultiInput", modify userInputParameters:
     Current: UIParameters: [{Id: "param-id", Value: ["Samsung"]}]
     Modified: UIParameters: [{Id: "param-id", Value: ["iPhone"]}] // Keep as array!
-> 4. update_existing_template_task(
     taskId: "abc123",
     userInputParameters: {
       UIParameters: [{Id: "param-id", Value: ["iPhone"]}], // Array with one element
       TemplateParameters: [{ParamName: "SearchKeyword", Value: ["iPhone"]}] // Array with one element
     },
     ...all_other_fields_from_step1
   )

Example 2: Update MultiInput parameter with multiple values
User: "Change keywords in task abc123 to 'iPhone', 'Samsung', and 'Google Pixel'"
-> 1. getTaskInfoById("abc123")
-> 2. Modify userInputParameters (check ControlType is MultiInput):
-> 3. update_existing_template_task(
     taskId: "abc123",
     userInputParameters: {
       UIParameters: [{Id: "param-id", Value: ["iPhone", "Samsung", "Google Pixel"]}], // Array
       TemplateParameters: [{ParamName: "SearchKeyword", Value: ["iPhone", "Samsung", "Google Pixel"]}] // Array
     },
     ...all_other_fields_from_step1
   )

Example 3: WRONG way - String instead of Array for MultiInput
❌ INCORRECT when updating MultiInput parameter:
-> Current: {Id: "param-id", Value: ["keyword1"]} // Correct format (array)
-> Updated: {Id: "param-id", Value: "iPhone"} // WRONG! Changed to string, will FAIL
✅ CORRECT:
-> Updated: {Id: "param-id", Value: ["iPhone"]} // Keep as array

Example 4: Rename task (no parameter changes)
User: "Rename task abc123 to 'My Amazon Scraper'"
-> 1. getTaskInfoById("abc123")
-> 2. update_existing_template_task(
     taskId: "abc123",
     taskName: "My Amazon Scraper",
     userInputParameters: {
       UIParameters: [/* preserve from step 1 - don't change */],
       TemplateParameters: [/* preserve from step 1 - don't change */]
     },
     ...other_fields_from_step1
   )

Example 5: Update mixed parameter types
User: "Change keywords to 'iPhone' and 'Samsung', set max pages to 20"
-> 1. getTaskInfoById("abc123")
-> 2. Check ControlTypes:
     - SearchKeyword: MultiInput -> Use array
     - MaxPages: NumberInput -> Use number
-> 3. update_existing_template_task(
     taskId: "abc123",
     userInputParameters: {
       UIParameters: [
         {Id: "param-id-1", Value: ["iPhone", "Samsung"]}, // MultiInput = array
         {Id: "param-id-2", Value: 20} // NumberInput = number
       ],
       TemplateParameters: [
         {ParamName: "SearchKeyword", Value: ["iPhone", "Samsung"]}, // Array
         {ParamName: "MaxPages", Value: 20} // Number
       ]
     },
     ...all_other_fields_from_step1
   )

User: "Create a task"
-> DO NOT call this tool, call createTemplateTask instead

taskId.description: REQUIRED: The ID of the task to update. Must be an existing task. Verify with getTaskInfoById first.

taskName.description: REQUIRED: The task name. Use current name from getTaskInfoById if not changing, or provide new name.

taskGroupId.description: REQUIRED: Task group ID. Use current value from getTaskInfoById if not changing group.

templateId.description: REQUIRED: Template ID. MUST match current template (cannot change template for existing task). Get from getTaskInfoById.

templateType.description: REQUIRED: Template type, usually 1. Get from getTaskInfoById. Do NOT change unless you know what you're doing.

templateVersion.description: REQUIRED: Current template version. Get from getTaskInfoById. If version mismatch, update will fail.

templateRegistrationId.description: REQUIRED: Template registration ID. Get from getTaskInfoById. This identifies the specific template instance.

Id.description: REQUIRED: Parameter ID from template schema. This uniquely identifies the parameter in the UI layer.

Value.description: OPTIONAL: Parameter value. Can be string, number, array, or object depending on parameter type.

sourceTaskId.description: OPTIONAL: Source task ID for task chaining

UIParameters.description: REQUIRED: UI-level parameters for Octoparse Desktop client display. Get current array from getTaskInfoById, modify only what needs to change. Each parameter in this array MUST have a corresponding entry in TemplateParameters with matching value.

ParamName.description: REQUIRED: Parameter name from template. This is the key used during task execution.

Value.description: OPTIONAL: Parameter value. Must match the corresponding UIParameter value.

TemplateParameters.description: REQUIRED: Template-level parameters for task execution. Get current array from getTaskInfoById, modify only what needs to change. Each parameter in this array MUST have a corresponding entry in UIParameters with matching value.

userInputParameters.description: REQUIRED: User input parameters object containing BOTH UIParameters and TemplateParameters arrays. CRITICAL: Both arrays are mandatory and parameters must be paired (every parameter in UIParameters must exist in TemplateParameters and vice versa). Get current parameters from getTaskInfoById, modify only what user wants to change while preserving all other parameters. If you modify a parameter value, you MUST update it in BOTH UIParameters and TemplateParameters to keep them paired. Do not omit any existing parameters or they will be lost.

templateVersionId.description: REQUIRED: Template version ID. Get from getTaskInfoById.

urlSourceTaskId.description: OPTIONAL: URL source task ID for task chaining. Preserve from getTaskInfoById if exists.

urlSourceTaskField.description: OPTIONAL: URL source field. Preserve from getTaskInfoById if exists.
